Title: Higher biomass accumulation by increasing phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase activity in Arabidopsis thaliana and Nicotiana tabacum

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in leaves and seedlings of transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: PRPP biosynthesis linked to nucleotide biosynthesis and nucleobase salvage
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic PRPP supply for nucleotide salvage and related PRPP-dependent metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ribose-5-phosphate and ATP into PRPP and AMP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as co-limiting for growth rate via PRPP supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nucleotide synthesis and salvage required for growth and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standardized liquid culture; soil growth under adequate or limiting nutrients; quartz sand culture; glasshouse low-light conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis and tobacco; Northern blot expression analysis; PRS enzyme activity assay; biomass measurements; metabolite profiling; [2-14C]uracil feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases PRS activity and substantially increases biomass accumulation in Arabidopsis thaliana and Nicotiana tabacum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive expression of fungal wild-type PRS increased total extractable PRS activity in Arabidopsis and tobacco. 2. Increased PRS activity positively correlated with seedling fresh weight and whole-plant biomass under multiple growth conditions. 3. Increased PRS activity was associated with reduced sucrose, increased hexose/sucrose ratio, and enhanced uracil salvage flux into insoluble nucleic-acid-containing fractions, supporting PRPP supply as a co-limiting factor for growth.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No consistent change in seed weight or seed fatty acid content; field performance and total seed yield not tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic engineering target to increase plant biomass and renewable energy feedstock production; deployment should be tested under field conditions and with yield assessment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The assay could not distinguish endogenous plant class I/class II PRS from heterologous Ashbya activity; direct PRPP quantification was unsuccessful, so PRPP increase was inferred indirectly from salvage flux and growth effects.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in leaves and seedlings of transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phosphoribosylpyrophosphate synthetase; mutant form resembling PRS class II-like allosteric behavior
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: PRPP biosynthesis linked to nucleotide biosynthesis and nucleobase salvage
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic PRPP supply for nucleotide salvage and related PRPP-dependent metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ribose-5-phosphate and ATP into PRPP and AMP; point mutations alter allosteric regulation by purine nucleotides and inorganic phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as co-limiting for growth rate via PRPP supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nucleotide synthesis and salvage required for growth and biomass accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standardized liquid culture; soil growth under adequate or limiting nutrients; quartz sand culture; glasshouse low-light conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis and tobacco; Northern blot expression analysis; PRS enzyme activity assay; biomass measurements; metabolite profiling; [2-14C]uracil feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases biomass accumulation, often more strongly than wild-type PRS despite a smaller increase in measured total PRS activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The mutant PRS carrying L133I and H196Q substitutions increased total PRS activity in Arabidopsis and tobacco. 2. Biomass enhancement in seedlings and plants was significant and in several cases greater than with wild-type PRS. 3. The superior growth effect despite lower total activity suggests kinetic/allosteric properties of the mutant enzyme influence metabolic flux and growth promotion.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No consistent change in seed weight or seed fatty acid content; altered allosteric regulation may have context-dependent metabolic effects not resolved here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially stronger engineering allele than wild-type PRS for biomass increase because modified allosteric regulation may improve flux; requires deeper kinetic and field evaluation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutations described as leucine 133 to isoleucine and histidine 196 to glutamine, causing altered inhibition by purine nucleotides and activation by inorganic phosphate; authors state it resembles PRS class II activity.
